output.executive_summary: March delivered 204 leads at a blended CPL of $119 — 82% of the 250-lead target, 21% under CPL target. Google Search is significantly outperforming at $94.7 CPL vs. $150 target. Meta cold is underperforming at $159 CPL and CTR declined 15% from February, suggesting early creative fatigue. Meta retargeting is a bright spot at $100 CPL. The primary action: shift $3k from Meta cold to Google Search this week and refresh Meta cold creative before April.

overall_metrics.vs_target: 204 vs 250 target (82% attainment); CPL $120 vs $150 target (20% under — good)
overall_metrics.vs_prior_period: +9% leads MoM (187 → 204); blended CPL slightly up ($113 → $120) driven by Meta cold degradation

top_performers.name: Google — SDR Enablement Terms
top_performers.why_top: High-intent search traffic. CTR of 4.0% on branded + category terms is strong. CPL is 37% below target — this channel has room to scale.

top_performers.name: Meta — Retargeting
top_performers.why_top: Warm audience converting efficiently at $100 CPL. Frequency at 2.8 — still room before saturation. Audience is small so can't scale significantly, but strong ROI.

anomalies.likely_cause: Creative fatigue. The VP Sales cold audience has seen this creative ~3.2x on average. The most responsive portion of the audience has already converted; remaining audience is less receptive. Recommend creative refresh before April.
Combined effect of creative fatigue (lower CTR) and possible increased competition for VP Sales audience in March (end of Q1 budget flush). CPL trending in wrong direction.

recommendations.action: Shift $3,000 from Meta cold budget to Google Search for April
recommendations.rationale: Google is delivering leads at $94.7 CPL vs. $150 target — 37% below target. Meta cold is at $159 and degrading. The budget shift leverages a proven channel while giving Meta cold time for creative refresh.
recommendations.estimated_impact: Based on current Google CPL, $3k additional spend = ~32 additional leads. Net April target achievable at 245-260 leads.

recommendations.action: Refresh Meta cold creative with 2 new variants (social proof angle + curiosity angle)
recommendations.rationale: Current creative has 3.2x average frequency with declining CTR — textbook creative fatigue. New creative resets the algorithm's optimization signal and re-engages the 80% of the audience that hasn't converted yet.
recommendations.estimated_impact: Creative refreshes typically recover 60-80% of prior CTR in the first 2 weeks. If CTR recovers to 0.82%, CPL returns to ~$142 range.

recommendations.action: Expand Meta cold audience by adding 1% lookalike based on March converters
recommendations.rationale: Current audience is showing saturation signals. Adding a fresh lookalike based on March's 78 converters introduces new high-quality inventory for the refreshed creative to work against.
recommendations.estimated_impact: Lookalike expansion typically improves CPL 10-15% vs. interest-only targeting for B2B audiences.

budget_reallocation.rationale: Shift $3k from underperforming Meta cold (CPL above target, degrading) to Google (CPL 37% below target, scalable). Keep retargeting budget flat — it's working efficiently and the audience can't absorb more.

output.attribution_notes: Meta attribution is 7-day click, 1-day view. Some Meta-attributed leads may have also touched a Google Search ad — blended CPL may slightly overstate Meta's contribution. For full-funnel attribution accuracy, recommend implementing UTM tracking to see multi-touch paths in HubSpot. Google last-click likely understates Google's contribution to brand search (which is driven by Meta awareness).
